name: Absurdist Corporate Training Video
vibe: Deadpan, low-budget 1990s VHS aesthetic where deeply unsettling or surreal concepts are explained using cheerful corporate buzzwords.

name: Neon Noir
vibe: Rain-slicked streets, cynical inner monologues, heavy synthwave ambiance, and high-contrast neon lighting masking moral decay.

name: High-Fantasy Mockumentary
vibe: Handheld cameras and awkward talking-head interviews, but the subjects are wizards, goblins, or mythological creatures dealing with mundane problems.

name: Gothic Soap Opera
vibe: Over-the-top melodrama, lingering stares, dramatic gasps, and complex family betrayals set in gloomy, candlelit, cobweb-filled manors.

name: Cassette Futurism Procedural
vibe: A gritty detective story set in a retro-future universe filled with chunky mechanical keyboards, floppy disks, green CRT monitors, and analog static.

name: Daylight Folk Horror
vibe: Bright, sunny, and deceptively idyllic rural settings where pagan rituals, smiling villagers, and unsettling isolation create slow-building dread.

name: Slapstick Bureaucracy
vibe: Fast-paced physical comedy and absurd escalations born entirely out of navigating ridiculously complex rules, paperwork, or office hierarchies.

name: Mumblecore Sci-Fi
vibe: Highly naturalistic, overlapping, improvised-feeling dialogue focusing on relationship drama, set against a backdrop of massive sci-fi events they largely ignore.

name: Corporate Cosmic Horror
vibe: The terrifying realization that the company you work for is controlled by, or actively worships, an ancient, incomprehensible, mind-shattering entity.

name: Late-Night Infomercial Fever Dream
vibe: Hyper-enthusiastic hosts, repetitive sales pitches, and garish graphics that slowly devolve into a hypnotic, nightmarish, or surreal psychological spiral.

name: Rust-Belt Magical Realism
vibe: Gritty, economically depressed industrial towns where everyday people treat subtle, unexplainable magic as just another mundane fact of life.

name: Balletic Gun-Fu
vibe: Hyper-stylized, meticulously choreographed combat that feels like a high-art dance piece, emphasizing rhythm, symmetry, and slow-motion elegance.

name: Analog Horror Sci-Fi
vibe: Found footage, distorted audio, and creepy public service announcements hiding a terrifying extraterrestrial or interdimensional threat.

name: Culinary Mockumentary
vibe: The intense, pretentious, high-stakes atmosphere of a Michelin-star chef documentary applied to completely ridiculous or low-brow subject matter.

name: Wuxia Office Politics
vibe: Standard corporate conflicts (stealing lunches, claiming credit) are treated with the epic gravity, wire-fu action, and honor codes of a martial arts epic.

name: Suburban Paranormal Found-Footage
vibe: Shaky smartphone or security cam footage in a sterile, boring suburban home where domestic peace is disrupted by terrifying, unseen poltergeist activity.

name: Passive-Aggressive Action Comedy
vibe: Characters engage in high-octane car chases and shootouts, but the dialogue is entirely focused on petty grievances and polite but biting insults.

name: Giallo-Inspired Fashion Slasher
vibe: Highly stylized, saturated primary colors, leather gloves, avant-garde electronic music, and glamorous settings masking brutal, stylized violence.

name: Biopunk Medical Drama
vibe: The high-pressure, fast-paced environment of an ER, but the doctors are dealing with runaway genetic modifications, organic tech, and body horror.

name: Deadpan Apocalyptic Sitcom
vibe: The world has ended in fire and ash, but the surviving characters just sit on a ruined couch making sarcastic, low-energy jokes about their new reality.

name: Steampunk Political Thriller
vibe: Brass, steam, and clockwork technology serving as the backdrop for tense espionage, back-room deals, and assassination plots.

name: Epistolary Romance (Digital)
vibe: A love story told entirely through the frantic, obsessive exchange of text messages, voicemails, and screen-recordings, with no face-to-face interaction.

name: Cold War Espionage Musical
vibe: Tense spy drop-offs, wiretaps, and double-agents, but the characters occasionally burst into highly choreographed, secretive musical numbers.

name: Psychedelic Road Movie
vibe: A journey down a long desert highway that becomes increasingly hallucinatory, colorful, and detached from reality the further they drive.

name: Utopian Propaganda Reel
vibe: Forced smiles, gleaming white architecture, and cheerful mid-Atlantic voiceovers hiding a sinister, heavily controlled dystopian reality.

name: Hardboiled Domestic Dispute
vibe: The cynical, poetic, hard-hitting dialogue of a 1930s pulp detective novel applied to a couple arguing over whose turn it is to do the dishes.

name: Kaiju Disaster Romance
vibe: Massive monsters are destroying a metropolitan city in the background, but the story is intimately focused on two people falling in love amidst the rubble.

name: 1980s Mall-Pop Coming-of-Age
vibe: Neon windbreakers, synth-pop montages, food courts, and the innocent, high-stakes emotional drama of high school crushes.

name: Sleep-Deprivation Surrealism
vibe: The blurry, illogical, time-skipping sensation of being awake for 72 hours, where shadows move and conversations stop making linear sense.

name: Cozy Murder Mystery
vibe: A charming, picturesque village filled with quirky locals who drink tea and knit, while a surprisingly brutal murder is solved by a busybody amateur.

name: Y2K Tech-Optimism Drama
vibe: Frosted tips, translucent electronics, dial-up sounds, and the naive, frantic excitement of the late 90s dot-com boom.

name: Neo-Victorian Ghost Story
vibe: Repressed emotions, strict etiquette, rigid corsets, and creeping psychological terror fueled by bumps in the night in a foggy London townhouse.

name: DIY Public Access Television
vibe: Zero-budget, awkward silences, terrible green screens, and earnest but incompetent hosts trying to broadcast a show to a local audience.

name: Hyper-Caffeinated Anime Parody
vibe: Screaming internal monologues, intense speed lines, dramatic camera angles, and exaggerated reactions to completely mundane events.

name: Urban Mythological Epic
vibe: Ancient gods and monsters living disguised in a modern, gritty metropolis, conducting epic battles in subway tunnels and dive bars.

name: Solarpunk Slice-of-Life
vibe: A bright, lush, sustainable future where technology and nature harmonize, focusing on gentle, low-stakes community problems and gardening.

name: Claustrophobic Creature Feature
vibe: Characters trapped in a tiny, locked environment (a submarine, a bunker) being slowly hunted by an unseen, deadly biological entity.

name: Stoner Heist
vibe: An incredibly complex, Ocean's 11-style robbery planned and executed by people who are deeply distracted, hungry, and confused.

name: Melodramatic Teen Soap
vibe: Beautiful 25-year-olds playing high schoolers, staring longingly into lockers, accompanied by moody indie-pop and devastating betrayal.

name: Deep Space Maritime Thriller
vibe: Spaceships treated like 18th-century naval galleons or WWII submarines, complete with sonar pings, creaking hulls, and stoic captains.

name: Psychological Eco-Terror
vibe: Nature itself becomes hostile and hallucinatory, using plants, spores, or animal behavior to slowly drive the human characters insane.

name: High-Society Farce
vibe: Rich aristocrats in tuxedos and gowns engaging in rapid-fire witty banter, mistaken identities, and frantic running through mansion doors.

name: Glitch-Art Existentialism
vibe: The fabric of reality keeps stuttering, freezing, or corrupting like a broken digital file as the protagonist questions their own existence.

name: Historical Revisionist Heist
vibe: A slick, modern heist movie structure, but set during a major historical event (e.g., stealing the Declaration of Independence while it's being signed).

name: AI Sentience Rom-Com
vibe: A bubbly, lighthearted romantic comedy where one of the leads is a highly logical, slightly awkward artificial intelligence learning to flirt.

name: Holiday Macabre
vibe: The cheerful music, bright lights, and forced joy of Christmas or Thanksgiving contrasted sharply against dark, violent, or cynical events.

name: Post-Scarcity Mumblecore
vibe: In a future where money doesn't exist and every need is met by robots, twenty-somethings still whine about aimlessness and artistic block.

name: Neo-Western Melodrama
vibe: Modern vehicles and clothes, but shot like a classic John Ford western with sweeping deserts, stoic staredowns, and themes of frontier justice.

name: Cybernetic Noir
vibe: A hardboiled detective story where memories can be hacked, limbs are mechanical, and the femme fatale is a rogue android.

name: Suburban Mid-Century Tragedy
vibe: Immaculate 1950s manicured lawns and pastel kitchens masking crushing psychological despair, alcoholism, and the suffocation of conformity.
